question_html:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
If there is a statement involving the natural number n such that

  1. The statement is true for n = 1
  2. When the statement is true for n = k (where k is some positive integer), then the statement is also true for n = k + 1.

Then, the statement is true for all natural numbers n.
Also, if A is a square matrix of order n, then A2 is defined as AA. In general, Am = AA .... A (m times), where m is any positive integer.

  1. If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then for any positive integer n, find An for n = 2. (1)
  2. If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find |An|, where n {tex}\in{/tex} N. (1)
  3. Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} and An = [aij]3{tex}\times{/tex}3 for some positive integer n, then find the cofactor of a13. (2)
    OR
    If A is a square matrix such that |A| = 2, then for any positive integer n, find |An|. (2)
question_text: Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow: If there is a statement involving the natural number n such that The statement is true for n = 1 When the statement is true for n = k (where k is some positive integer), then the statement is also true for n = k + 1. Then, the statement is true for all natural numbers n. Also, if A is a square matrix of order n, then A 2 is defined as AA. In general, A m = AA .... A (m times), where m is any positive integer. If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then for any positive integer n, find A n for n = 2. (1) If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find |A n |, where n {tex}\in{/tex} N. (1) Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A n = [a ij ] 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 for some positive integer n, then find the cofactor of a 13 . (2) OR If A is a square matrix such that |A| = 2, then for any positive integer n, find |A n |. (2)
explanation: We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -8 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , which can be obtained from A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1+2 n & -4 n \\ n & 1-2 n \end{array}\right]{/tex} for n = 2. We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1 - 0 = 1 Also, |A n | = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n times)| = |A| n = 1 n = 1 We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{2} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{2} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{n} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{n} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{n} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, cofactor of a 13 = (-1) 1+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & a^{n} \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0 OR We have, |A| = 2 and |A n | = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n-times)| = |A| |A| ... |A| (n-times) = |A| n = 2 n
solution: We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -8 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , which can be obtained from A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1+2 n & -4 n \\ n & 1-2 n \end{array}\right]{/tex} for n = 2. We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1 - 0 = 1 Also, |A n | = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n times)| = |A| n = 1 n = 1 We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{2} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{2} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{n} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{n} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{n} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, cofactor of a 13 = (-1) 1+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & a^{n} \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0 OR We have, |A| = 2 and |A n | = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n-times)| = |A| |A| ... |A| (n-times) = |A| n = 2 n
solution_html:
1. We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} A2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -8 \\ 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, which can be obtained from An = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1+2 n & -4 n \\ n & 1-2 n \end{array}\right]{/tex}for n = 2.

  2. We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1 - 0 = 1
    Also, |An| = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n times)| = |A|n = 1n = 1

  3. We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} A2 = A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} a & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{2} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{2} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    Similarly, An = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} a^{n} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & a^{n} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & a^{n} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    Now, cofactor of a13 = (-1)1+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & a^{n} \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0
    OR
    We have, |A| = 2
    and |An| = |A {tex}\cdot{/tex} A ...A (n-times)|
    = |A| |A| ... |A| (n-times) = |A|n = 2n

question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = -5; x + y - 2z = -3
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Using A -1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = -5; x + y - 2z = -3
explanation: Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) - (-3)(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2) = 0 - 6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \ne{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 exists and A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} (adj. A)...(i) Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 and A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 and A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23, A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2&1 \\ { - 1}&{ - 9}&{ - 5} \\ 2&{23}&{13} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3

A)...(i) Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 and A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 and A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23, A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. (i), A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3
solution_html:
Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) - (-3)(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2) = 0 - 6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \ne{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A-1 exists and A-1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} (adj. A)...(i)
Now, A11 = 0, A12 = 2, A13 = 1 and A21 = -1, A22 = -9, A23 = -5 and A31 = 2, A32 = 23, A33 = 13
{tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. A ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2&1 \\ { - 1}&{ - 9}&{ - 5} \\ 2&{23}&{13} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
From eq. (i),
A-1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Here A ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Therefore, solution is unique and X = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3

question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A-1, using A-1 solve the system of equations
2x - 3y + 5z = 11
3x + 2y - 4z = -5
x + y - 2z = -3
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A -1 , using A -1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 x + y - 2z = -3
explanation: A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1 Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23,A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ....(1) Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ] {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​
solution: A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1 Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23,A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ....(1) Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ] {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1
Now, A11 = 0, A12= 2, A13= 1
A21= -1, A22 = -9, A23= -5
A31= 2, A32= 23,A33 = 13

 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}....(1)

Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A-1B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ]

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​

question_html: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, show that A3 - 6A2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A-1.
question_text: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , show that A 3 - 6A 2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A -1 .
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 1 + 2}&{1 + 2 - 1}&{1 - 3 + 3} \\ {1 + 2 - 6}&{1 + 4 + 3}&{1 - 6 - 9} \\ {2 - 1 + 6}&{2 - 2 - 3}&{2 + 3 + 9} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now {tex}{A^3} = {A^2}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {4 + 2 + 2}&{4 + 4 + 1}&{4 - 6 + 3} \\ { - 3 + 8 - 28}&{ - 3 + 16 + 14}&{ - 3 - 24 - 42} \\ {7 - 3 + 28}&{7 - 6 - 14}&{7 + 9 + 42} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

L.H.S. {tex} = {A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right] + {/tex}{tex}5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] + 11\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {24}&{12}&6 \\ { - 18}&{48}&{ - 84} \\ {42}&{ - 18}&{84} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&5&5 \\ 5&{10}&{ - 15} \\ {10}&{ - 5}&{15} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {8 - 24 + 5}&{7 - 12 + 5}&{1 - 6 + 5} \\ { - 23 + 18 + 5}&{27 - 48 + 10}&{ - 69 + 84 - 15} \\ {32 - 42 + 10}&{ - 13 + 18 - 5}&{58 + 84 + 15} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 11}&0&0 \\ 0&{ - 11}&0 \\ 0&0&{ - 11} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}= R.H.S.

Now,   to find {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}, multiplying {tex}{A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I = 0{/tex} by {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^3}{A^{ - 1}} - 6{A^2}{A^{ - 1}} + 5A{A^{ - 1}} + 11I.{A^{ - 1}} = 0.{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} - 6A + 5I + 11{A^{ - 1}} = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6A - 5I - {A^2}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&6&6 \\ 6&{12}&{ - 18} \\ {12}&{ - 6}&{18} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&0&0 \\ 0&5&0 \\ 0&0&5 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 5 - 4}&{6 - 2}&{6 - 1} \\ {6 + 3}&{12 - 5 - 8}&{ - 18 + 14} \\ {12 - 7}&{ - 6 + 3}&{18 - 5 - 14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i) Also, we have the system of linear equations as {tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex} {tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex} and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex} Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B, where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex} Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by, {tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3{/tex}
solution: We have, {tex}A = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = 1\left( { - 3} \right) - 2\left( { - 2} \right) + 0 = 1 \ne 0{/tex} Now, {tex}{A_{11}} = - 3,{A_{12}} = 2,{A_{13}} = 2,{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - 2,{A_{22}} = 1,{A_{23}} = 1,{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = - 4,{A_{32}} = 2{/tex} and {tex}{A_{33}} = 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj\left( A \right) = {\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right|^T} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adjA}}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i) Also, we have the system of linear equations as {tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex} {tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex} and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex} Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B, where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex} Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by, {tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 30 + 60 + 14} \\ { - 20 + 8 + 7} \\ { - 40 + 16 + 21} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore x = 0,y = - 5{/tex} and {tex}z = - solution_html:
We have, {tex}A = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}...(i)

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = 1\left( { - 3} \right) - 2\left( { - 2} \right) + 0 = 1 \ne 0{/tex}

Now, {tex}{A_{11}} = - 3,{A_{12}} = 2,{A_{13}} = 2,{/tex}{tex}{A_{21}} = - 2,{A_{22}} = 1,{A_{23}} = 1,{/tex}{tex}{A_{31}} = - 4,{A_{32}} = 2{/tex} and {tex}{A_{33}} = 3{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adj\left( A \right) = {\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right|^T} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adjA}}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i)

Also, we have the system of linear equations as

{tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex}

{tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex}

and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex}

Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B,

where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{/tex}Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by,

{tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 30 + 60 + 14} \\ { - 20 + 8 + 7} \\ { - 40 + 16 + 21} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore x = 0,y = - 5{/tex} and {tex}z = - 3{/tex}

question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1, solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Using A -1 , solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9
explanation: {tex}|A|{/tex} = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 {tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists Now, A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -5 A 21 = 1, A 22 = -6, A 23 = 3 A 31 = -1 , A 32 = 2, A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations are y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B, where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now AX = B ⇒ X = A -1 B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution: {tex}|A|{/tex} = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 {tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists Now, A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -5 A 21 = 1, A 22 = -6, A 23 = 3 A 31 = -1 , A 32 = 2, A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations are y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B, where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now AX = B ⇒ X = A -1 B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution_html:
{tex}|A|{/tex}= {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 

{tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 exists
Now, A11 = -1, A12 = 8, A13 = -5
A21 = 1, A22 = -6, A23 = 3
A31 = -1 , A32 = 2, A33 = -1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex}adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations are

y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9

It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B,
where A ={tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now AX = B ⇒ X = A-1B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2

question_html:
Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equation. 

x - y + z = 4

x - 2y - 2z = 9

2x + y + 3z = 1
question_text: Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equation. x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1
explanation: x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1 Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} X = A -1 C {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 3, y = -2. z = -1
solution: x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1 Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} X = A -1 C {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 3, y = -2. z = -1
solution_html:
x - y + z = 4

x - 2y - 2z = 9

2x + y + 3z = 1

Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
AX = C

{tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

AB = 8I 
{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}

 {tex} = \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

X = A-1C

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

x = 3, y = -2. z = -1

question_html:
Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations:
x + 2y -3z = -4
2x + 3y + 2z = 2
3x - 3y - 3z = 11
question_text: Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations: x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11
explanation: Given system of linear equations are x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} |A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66 {tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B A 11= -3 , A 12 ​= 12 , ​​​​​​A 13 ​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A 2 1 ​= 15 , ​​​​​​A 22 ​= 6, ​​​​​​A 23 ​= 9, ​​​​​​A 31 ​ = 13, ​​​​​​A 32 ​ = -8, ​​​​​​A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex} , y = - {tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}
solution: Given system of linear equations are x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} |A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66 {tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B A 11= -3 , A 12 ​= 12 , ​​​​​​A 13 ​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A 2 1 ​= 15 , ​​​​​​A 22 ​= 6, ​​​​​​A 23 ​= 9, ​​​​​​A 31 ​ = 13, ​​​​​​A 32 ​ = -8, ​​​​​​A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex} , y = - {tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given system of linear equations are
x + 2y -3z = -4
2x + 3y + 2z = 2
3x - 3y - 3z = 11

It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} 
or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex}, X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex}and B ={tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} 

|A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66

{tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A-1 B
A11= -3 , A12​= 12 , ​​​​​​A13​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A21​= 15 , ​​​​​​A22​= 6, ​​​​​​A23​= 9, ​​​​​​A31​ = 13, ​​​​​​A32​ = -8, ​​​​​​A33 = -1

adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

So X ={tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

= {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex}, y = -{tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex}, z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}

question_html: Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right],B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, find BA and use this to solve the system of equations y + 2z = 7, x - y = 3, 2x + 3y + 4z = 17.
question_text: Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right],B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , find BA and use this to solve the system of equations y + 2z = 7, x - y = 3, 2x + 3y + 4z = 17.
explanation: We have, A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} BA = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&0&0 \\ 0&6&0 \\ 0&0&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} = 6I {tex}\therefore{/tex} B -1 = {tex}\frac{A}{6}=\frac{1}{6}{/tex} A = {tex} \frac{1}{6}\left[{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Also, x - y = 3, 2x + 3y + 4z = 17 and y + 2z = 7 {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [using Eq. (i)] {tex} = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 + 34 - 28} \\ { - 12 + 34 - 28} \\ {6 - 17 + 35} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12} \\ { - 6} \\ {24} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} x = 2, y = -1 and z = 4

(i)] {tex} = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 + 34 - 28} \\ { - 12 + 34 - 28} \\ {6 - 17 + 35} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12} \\ { - 6} \\ {24} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} x = 2, y = -1 and z = 4
solution_html:
We have, A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} BA  = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&0&0 \\ 0&6&0 \\ 0&0&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} = 6I
{tex}\therefore{/tex} B-1 = {tex}\frac{A}{6}=\frac{1}{6}{/tex} A = {tex} \frac{1}{6}\left[{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Also, x - y = 3, 2x + 3y + 4z = 17 and y + 2z = 7
{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&0 \\ 2&3&4 \\ 0&1&2 \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2&{ - 4} \\ { - 4}&2&{ - 4} \\ 2&{ - 1}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ {17} \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}[using Eq. (i)]
{tex} = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 + 34 - 28} \\ { - 12 + 34 - 28} \\ {6 - 17 + 35} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{6}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12} \\ { - 6} \\ {24} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore {/tex} x = 2, y = -1 and z = 4

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & -1 & 2 \\ 7 & 3 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find {tex}A^{-1}{/tex}. Using {tex}A^{-1}{/tex}, solve the given system of equations {tex}3 {x}+4 {y}+7 {z}=14 ; 2 {x}-{y}+3 {z}{/tex} {tex}=4 ; x+2 {y}-3 {z}=0{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & -1 & 2 \\ 7 & 3 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find {tex}A^{-1}{/tex} . Using {tex}A^{-1}{/tex} , solve the given system of equations {tex}3 {x}+4 {y}+7 {z}=14 ; 2 {x}-{y}+3 {z}{/tex} {tex}=4 ; x+2 {y}-3 {z}=0{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex} where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}
solution: {tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex} where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists.
cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex}
where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}

question_html:
Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ -7&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equations.

{tex}x - y + z = 4{/tex}

{tex}x - 2y - 2z = 9{/tex}

{tex}2x + y + 3z = 1{/tex}
question_text: Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ -7&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equations. {tex}x - y + z = 4{/tex} {tex}x - 2y - 2z = 9{/tex} {tex}2x + y + 3z = 1{/tex}
explanation: {tex}x - y + z = 4{/tex} {tex}x - 2y - 2z = 9{/tex} {tex}2x + y + 3z = 1{/tex} Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Then, given system of equations can be rewritten as, AX = C Now, {tex}A B= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A^{-1}=\frac18\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}{/tex} Now, AX = C, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 C {tex}\Rightarrow\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}4\\9\\1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\;=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-4}2+\frac92+\frac12\\\frac{-28}8+\frac98+\frac38\\\frac{20}8+\frac{-27}8+\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}3\\-2\\-1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=3,y=-2,z=-1{/tex}
solution: {tex}x - y + z = 4{/tex} {tex}x - 2y - 2z = 9{/tex} {tex}2x + y + 3z = 1{/tex} Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Then, given system of equations can be rewritten as, AX = C Now, {tex}A B= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A^{-1}=\frac18\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}{/tex} Now, AX = C, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 C {tex}\Rightarrow\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}4\\9\\1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\;=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-4}2+\frac92+\frac12\\\frac{-28}8+\frac98+\frac38\\\frac{20}8+\frac{-27}8+\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}3\\-2\\-1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=3,y=-2,z=-1{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}x - y + z = 4{/tex}

{tex}x - 2y - 2z = 9{/tex}

{tex}2x + y + 3z = 1{/tex}

Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Then, given system of equations can be rewritten as,
AX = C

  Now,  {tex}A B= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

AB = 8I
{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow A^{-1}=\frac18\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

Now, AX = C,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A-1C
{tex}\Rightarrow\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-1}2&\frac12&\frac12\\\frac{-7}8&\frac18&\frac38\\\frac58&\frac{-3}8&\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}4\\9\\1\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

{tex}\;=\begin{bmatrix}\frac{-4}2+\frac92+\frac12\\\frac{-28}8+\frac98+\frac38\\\frac{20}8+\frac{-27}8+\frac{-1}8\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}3\\-2\\-1\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow x=3,y=-2,z=-1{/tex}

and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \cos \alpha } & { - \sin \alpha } & { 0 } \\ { \sin \alpha } & { \cos \alpha } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
= 1·(cos2{tex} \alpha{/tex} + sin2{tex} \alpha{/tex}) = 1 [expanding along R3]

question_html:
Solve the system of equations
{tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex}
{tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex}
{tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
question_text: Solve the system of equations {tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex} {tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex} {tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} 2u + 3v + 10w = 4 4u – 6v+ 5w = 1 6u + 9v – 20w = 2 {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36] = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A is non-singular and hence A -1 exists. Now, A 11 = 75, A 12 = 110, A 13 = 72 A 21 = 150, A 22 = -100, A 23 = 0 A 31 = 75, A 32 = 30, A 33 = -2 {tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
solution: Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} 2u + 3v + 10w = 4 4u – 6v+ 5w = 1 6u + 9v – 20w = 2 {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36] = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A is non-singular and hence A -1 exists. Now, A 11 = 75, A 12 = 110, A 13 = 72 A 21 = 150, A 22 = -100, A 23 = 0 A 31 = 75, A 32 = 30, A 33 = -2 {tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
solution_html:
Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex}
2u + 3v + 10w = 4
4u – 6v+ 5w = 1
6u + 9v – 20w = 2
{tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex}

= 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36]

= 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200{tex} \neq0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex}A is non-singular and hence A-1 exists.

Now, A11 = 75, A12 = 110, A13 = 72
A21 = 150, A22 = -100, A23 = 0
A31 = 75, A32 = 30, A33 = -2

{tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

{tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

x = 2, y = 3, z = 5

question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, verify that A3 - 6A2 + 9A - 4I = 0 and hence find A-1
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , verify that A 3 - 6A 2 + 9A - 4I = 0 and hence find A -1
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&-1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&-1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^2}=\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {4 + 1 + 1}&{ - 2 - 2 - 1}&{2 + 1 + 2} \\ { - 2 - 2 - 1}&{1 + 4 + 1}&{ - 1 - 2 - 2} \\ {2 + 1 + 2}&{ - 1 - 2 - 2}&{1 + 1 + 4} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now A3 = A2.A  {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12 + 5 + 5}&{ - 6 - 10 - 5}&{6 + 5 + 10} \\ { - 10 - 6 - 5}&{5 + 12 + 5}&{ - 5 - 6 - 10} \\ {10 + 5 + 6}&{ - 5 - 10 - 6}&{5 + 5 + 12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

L.H.S. {tex} = {A^3} - 6{A^2} + 9A - 4I{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right] - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ 9\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] - 4\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {36}&{ - 30}&{30} \\ { - 30}&{36}&{ - 30} \\ {30}&{ - 30}&{36} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18}&{ - 9}&9 \\ { - 9}&{18}&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 9}&{18} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&0&0 \\ 0&4&0 \\ 0&0&4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22 - 36}&{ - 21 + 30}&{21 - 30} \\ { - 21 + 30}&{22 - 36}&{ - 21 + 30} \\ {21 - 30}&{ - 21 + 30}&{22 - 36} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18 - 4}&{ - 9 - 0}&{9 - 0} \\ { - 9 - 0}&{18 - 4}&{ - 9 - 0} \\ {9 - 0}&{ - 9 - 0}&{18 - 4} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 14}&9&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 14}&9 \\ { - 9}&9&{14} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 14}&9&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 14}&9 \\ { - 9}&9&{14} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}= R.H.S.

Now,   to find {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}, multiplying {tex}{A^3} - 6{A^2} + 9A - 4{I^{ - 1}} = 0.{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}by {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^3}{A^{ - 1}} - 6{A^2}{A^{ - 1}} + 9A{A^{ - 1}} - 4I.{A^{ - 1}} = 0{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} - 6A + 9I - 4{A^{ - 1}} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A}^{-1} = {A^2} - 6A + 9I{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A}^{-1} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] + 9\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12}&{ - 6}&6 \\ { - 6}&{12}&{ - 6} \\ 6&{ - 6}&{12} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&0&0 \\ 0&9&0 \\ 0&0&9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 12 + 9}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0}&{5 - 6 + 0} \\ { - 5 + 6 + 0}&{6 - 12 + 9}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0} \\ {5 - 6 + 0}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0}&{6 - 12 + 9} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&{ - 1} \\ 1&3&1 \\ { - 1}&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&{ - 1} \\ 1&3&1 \\ { - 1}&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_text: If A= {tex} \left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find A -1 and hence solve the system of equations x+ 2y+z= 4 -x+y+z=0 and x- 3y + z = 4.
explanation: Given, A= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, |A| = 1 (1 + 3) - 2 ( - 1 -1) + 1 ( 3-1) = 4 + 4 + 2=10 {tex}\Rightarrow \quad | A | \neq 0{/tex} , hence A -1 exists. solution_html:
Given, A= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = 1 (1 + 3) - 2 ( - 1 -1) + 1 ( 3-1)
= 4 + 4 + 2=10
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad | A | \neq 0{/tex}, hence A-1 exists.
Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are,
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 + 3 ) = 4{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } \end{array} \right| = ( 3 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 2 + 3 ) = - 5{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 - 1 ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 3 - 2 ) = 5{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 2 - 1 ) = 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 1 + 1 ) = - 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 + 2 ) = 3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { 2 } & { 2 } \\ { - 5 } & { 0 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]^T = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \operatorname { adj } ( A ){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written in matrix from as
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
i.e.  AX = B
where,
{tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] B = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right] \text { and } X = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Clearly, {tex}X = A ^ { - 1 } B = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 16 + 0 + 4 } \\ { 8 + 0 + ( - 8 ) } \\ { 8 + 0 + 12 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 20 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 20 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 2, y = 0 and z = 0

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the following system of equations
{tex}\frac { 2 } { x } + \frac { 3 } { y } + \frac { 10 } { z } = 4{/tex}
{tex}\frac { 4 } { x } - \frac { 6 } { y } + \frac { 5 } { z } = 1{/tex}
and {tex}\frac { 6 } { x } + \frac { 9 } { y } - \frac { 20 } { z } = 2 ,where\ x , y , z \neq 0{/tex}.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the following system of equations {tex}\frac { 2 } { x } + \frac { 3 } { y } + \frac { 10 } { z } = 4{/tex} {tex}\frac { 4 } { x } - \frac { 6 } { y } + \frac { 5 } { z } = 1{/tex} and {tex}\frac { 6 } { x } + \frac { 9 } { y } - \frac { 20 } { z } = 2 ,where\ x , y , z \neq 0{/tex} .
explanation: Given system of equations is {tex}\frac { 2 } { x } + \frac { 3 } { y } + \frac { 10 } { z } = 4 , \frac { 4 } { x } - \frac { 6 } { y } + \frac { 5 } { z } = 1{/tex} and {tex}\frac { 6 } { x } + \frac { 9 } { y } - \frac { 20 } { z } = 2 ; x , y , z \neq 0{/tex} We shall make use of substitutions in order to get the required result. Let {tex}\frac { 1 } { x } = u , \frac { 1 } { y } = v \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = w{/tex} , then given system of equations reduces to , {tex}\left. \begin{array} { r } { 2 u + 3 v + 10 w = 4 } \\ { 4 u - 6 v + 5 w = 1 } \\ { \text { and } \quad 6 u + 9 v - 20 w = 2 } \end{array} \right\}{/tex} ...........(i) Therefore,Above system of Eqs. (i) can be written in matrix form as AX = B, where, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 2 } & { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { 4 } & { - 6 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right] B = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right] \text { and } X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Its solution is given by X = A - 1 B Here, |A| = 2 (120 - 45) - 3 (-80 - 30) + 10 (36 + 36) = 2(75) -3 (-110) + 10(72) = 150 + 330 + 720= 1200 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= 1200 Since, |A| {tex}\neq {/tex} 0, so unique solution exists. Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are, {tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { - 6 } & { 5 } \\ { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 120 - 45 ) = 75{/tex} {tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 80 - 30 ) = 110{/tex} {tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { - 6 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 36 + 36 ) = 72{/tex} {tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 60 - 90 ) = 150{/tex} {tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 6 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 40 - 60 ) = - 100{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 18 - 18 ) = 0{/tex} {tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { - 6 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 15 + 60 ) = 75{/tex} {tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 4 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 10 - 40 ) = 30{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 4 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 12 - 12 ) = - 24{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 110 } & { 72 } \\ { 150 } & { - 100 } & { 0 } \\ { 75 } & { 30 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore,on putting the values of X, A -1 and B in eq. (ii), we get, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 300 + 150 + 150 } \\ { 440 - 100 + 60 } \\ { 288 + 0 - 48 } \end{array} \right] \Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 600 } \\ { 400 } \\ { 240 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get, {tex}u = \frac { 600 } { 1200 } , v = \frac { 400 } { 1200 } , w = \frac { 240 } { 1200 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad u = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , v = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } w = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex} But {tex}\frac { 1 } { x } = u , \frac { 1 } { y } = v \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = w{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 1 } { y } = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 3 and z = 5
