6 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 36 + 36 ) = 72{/tex} {tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 60 - 90 ) = 150{/tex} {tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 6 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 40 - 60 ) = - 100{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 18 - 18 ) = 0{/tex} {tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { - 6 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 15 + 60 ) = 75{/tex} {tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 4 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 10 - 40 ) = 30{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 4 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 12 - 12 ) = - 24{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 110 } & { 72 } \\ { 150 } & { - 100 } & { 0 } \\ { 75 } & { 30 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore,on putting the values of X, A -1 and B in eq. (ii), we get, {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 300 + 150 + 150 } \\ { 440 - 100 + 60 } \\ { 288 + 0 - 48 } \end{array} \right] \Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 600 } \\ { 400 } \\ { 240 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get, {tex}u = \frac { 600 } { 1200 } , v = \frac { 400 } { 1200 } , w = \frac { 240 } { 1200 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad u = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , v = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } w = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex} But {tex}\frac { 1 } { x } = u , \frac { 1 } { y } = v \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = w{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 1 } { y } = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 3 and z = 5
solution_html:
Given system of equations is
{tex}\frac { 2 } { x } + \frac { 3 } { y } + \frac { 10 } { z } = 4 , \frac { 4 } { x } - \frac { 6 } { y } + \frac { 5 } { z } = 1{/tex}
and {tex}\frac { 6 } { x } + \frac { 9 } { y } - \frac { 20 } { z } = 2 ; x , y , z \neq 0{/tex}

We shall make use of substitutions in order to get the required  result.
Let {tex}\frac { 1 } { x } = u , \frac { 1 } { y } = v \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = w{/tex},
then  given system of equations reduces to ,
{tex}\left. \begin{array} { r } { 2 u + 3 v + 10 w = 4 } \\ { 4 u - 6 v + 5 w = 1 } \\ { \text { and } \quad 6 u + 9 v - 20 w = 2 } \end{array} \right\}{/tex}

...........(i)
Therefore,Above system of Eqs. (i) can be written in matrix form as AX = B, where,
{tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 2 } & { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { 4 } & { - 6 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right] B = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right] \text { and } X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Its solution is given by
X = A- 1B
Here, |A| = 2 (120 - 45) - 3 (-80 - 30) + 10 (36 + 36)
= 2(75) -3 (-110) + 10(72)
= 150 + 330 + 720= 1200
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= 1200
Since, |A|{tex}\neq {/tex} 0, so unique solution exists. Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are,
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { - 6 } & { 5 } \\ { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 120 - 45 ) = 75{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 80 - 30 ) = 110{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { - 6 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 36 + 36 ) = 72{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { 9 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 60 - 90 ) = 150{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 6 } & { - 20 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 40 - 60 ) = - 100{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 6 } & { 9 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 18 - 18 ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { 10 } \\ { - 6 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 15 + 60 ) = 75{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 10 } \\ { 4 } & { 5 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 10 - 40 ) = 30{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 4 } & { - 6 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 12 - 12 ) = - 24{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 110 } & { 72 } \\ { 150 } & { - 100 } & { 0 } \\ { 75 } & { 30 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on putting the values of X, A-1 and B in eq. (ii),
we get,
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 75 } & { 150 } & { 75 } \\ { 110 } & { - 100 } & { 30 } \\ { 72 } & { 0 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 300 + 150 + 150 } \\ { 440 - 100 + 60 } \\ { 288 + 0 - 48 } \end{array} \right] \Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { u } \\ { v } \\ { w } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 1200 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 600 } \\ { 400 } \\ { 240 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get,
{tex}u = \frac { 600 } { 1200 } , v = \frac { 400 } { 1200 } , w = \frac { 240 } { 1200 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad u = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , v = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } w = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex}
But {tex}\frac { 1 } { x } = u , \frac { 1 } { y } = v \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = w{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 1 } { y } = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \text { and } \frac { 1 } { z } = \frac { 1 } { 5 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 3 and z = 5

question_html:
Solve the system of the following equations: (Using matrices):

{tex}\frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4;{/tex}{tex}\frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1;{/tex}{tex}\frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{20}}{z} = 2;{/tex}
question_text: Solve the system of the following equations: (Using matrices): {tex}\frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4;{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1;{/tex} {tex}\frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{20}}{z} = 2;{/tex}
explanation: Put {tex}\frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} in the given equations, {tex}2u + 3v + 10w = 4;{/tex} {tex}4u - 6v + 5w = 1;{/tex} {tex}6u + 9v - 20w = 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix form of given equations is {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [AX= B] Here, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {120 - 45} \right) - 3\left( { - 80 - 30} \right) + 10\left( {36 + 36} \right){/tex} {tex} = 150 + 330 + 750 = 1200 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists and unique solution is {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} ….(i) Now {tex}{A_{11}} = 75,{A_{12}} = 110,{A_{13}} = 72{/tex} and {tex}{A_{21}} = 150,{A_{22}} = - 100,{A_{23}} = 0{/tex} and {tex}{A_{31}} = 75,{A_{32}} = 30,{A_{33}} = - 24{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{110}&{72} \\ {150}&{ - 100}&0 \\ {75}&{30}&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} And {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adj.A}}{{\left| A \right|}} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {300 + 150 + 150} \\ {440 - 100 + 60} \\ {288 + 0 - 48} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{1}{u} = 2,y = \frac{1}{v} = 3,z = \frac{1}{w} = 5{/tex}
solution: Put {tex}\frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} in the given equations, {tex}2u + 3v + 10w = 4;{/tex} {tex}4u - 6v + 5w = 1;{/tex} {tex}6u + 9v - 20w = 2{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix form of given equations is {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [AX= B] Here, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {120 - 45} \right) - 3\left( { - 80 - 30} \right) + 10\left( {36 + 36} \right){/tex} {tex} = 150 + 330 + 750 = 1200 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists and unique solution is {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} ….(i) Now {tex}{A_{11}} = 75,{A_{12}} = 110,{A_{13}} = 72{/tex} and {tex}{A_{21}} = 150,{A_{22}} = - 100,{A_{23}} = 0{/tex} and {tex}{A_{31}} = 75,{A_{32}} = 30,{A_{33}} = - 24{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{110}&{72} \\ {150}&{ - 100}&0 \\ {75}&{30}&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} And {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adj.A}}{{\left| A \right|}} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {300 + 150 + 150} \\ {440 - 100 + 60} \\ {288 + 0 - 48} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{1}{u} = 2,y = \frac{1}{v} = 3,z = \frac{1}{w} = 5{/tex}
solution_html:
Put {tex}\frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} in the given equations,

{tex}2u + 3v + 10w = 4;{/tex} {tex}4u - 6v + 5w = 1;{/tex} {tex}6u + 9v - 20w = 2{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} The matrix form of given equations is {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}[AX= B]

Here, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ v \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}= 2\left( {120 - 45} \right) - 3\left( { - 80 - 30} \right) + 10\left( {36 + 36} \right){/tex}

{tex} = 150 + 330 + 750 = 1200 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex}exists and unique solution is {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}….(i)

Now {tex}{A_{11}} = 75,{A_{12}} = 110,{A_{13}} = 72{/tex} and {tex}{A_{21}} = 150,{A_{22}} = - 100,{A_{23}} = 0{/tex} and {tex}{A_{31}} = 75,{A_{32}} = 30,{A_{33}} = - 24{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{110}&{72} \\ {150}&{ - 100}&0 \\ {75}&{30}&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

And {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adj.A}}{{\left| A \right|}} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {300 + 150 + 150} \\ {440 - 100 + 60} \\ {288 + 0 - 48} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{1}{u} = 2,y = \frac{1}{v} = 3,z = \frac{1}{w} = 5{/tex}

question_html: Show that the matrix, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} satisfies the equation, A3 – A2 – 3A – I3 = O. Hence, find A-1
question_text: Show that the matrix, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} satisfies the equation, A 3 – A 2 – 3A – I 3 = O. Hence, find A -1
solution_html:
Here, we have:
{tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A3 = A2.A
{tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1+0-6} & {0+0-8} & {-2+0-2} \\ {-2+2+6} & {0+1+8} & {4-2+2} \\ {3-8+3} & {0-4+4} & {-6+8+1} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5} & {-8} & {-4} \\ {6} & {9} & {4} \\ {-2} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A2.A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5} & {-8} & {-4} \\ {6} & {9} & {4} \\ {-2} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5^{2}+16-12} & {0-8+16} & {10-16-4} \\ {6-18+12} & {0-9+16} & {-12+18+4} \\ {-2-0+9} & {0-0-12} & {4+0+3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-8} & {-10} \\ {0} & {7} & {10} \\ {7} & {12} & {7} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, A3 – A2 – 3A – I
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-8} & {-10} \\ {0} & {7} & {10} \\ {7} & {12} & {7} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5} & {-8} & {-4} \\ {6} & {9} & {4} \\ {-2} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]-3\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {1} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1+5} & {-8+8} & {-10+4} \\ {0-6} & {7-9} & {10-4} \\ {7+2} & {12-0} & {7-3} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-3-1} & {-0-0} & {6-0} \\ {6-0} & {+3-1} & {-6-0} \\ {-9-0} & {-12+0} & {-3-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {4} & {0} & {-6} \\ {-6} & {-2} & {6} \\ {9} & {12} & {4} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-4} & {0} & {6} \\ {6} & {2} & {-6} \\ {-9} & {-12} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {0} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {0} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Thus, A3 – A2 – 3A – I = 0
Multiply both sides by {tex}A^{-1}{/tex},  we get
{tex}A^{-1}A^3 – A^{-1}A^2 – 3A^{-1}A – IA^{-1} = 0{/tex}
A2 – A – 3I = {tex}A^{-1}{/tex}   ...(since {tex}A^{-1}A= I{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A – 1 = (A2 - A - 3I)
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-5} & {-8} & {-4} \\ {6} & {9} & {4} \\ {-2} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]-3\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {1} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5} & {-8} & {-4} \\ {6} & {9} & {4} \\ {-2} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {0} & {-2} \\ {-2} & {-1} & {2} \\ {3} & {4} & {1} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {3} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-5-1-3} & {-8-0-0} & {-4+2-0} \\ {6+2-0} & {7+1-3} & {4-2-0} \\ {-2-3-0} & {0-4-0} & {3-1-3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-9} & {-8} & {-2} \\ {8} & {7} & {2} \\ {-5} & {-4} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, A – 1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-9} & {-8} & {-2} \\ {8} & {7} & {2} \\ {-5} & {-4} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: The sum of three numbers is 6. If we multiply third number by 3 and add second number to it, we get 11. By adding first and third numbers, we get double of the second number. Represent it algebraically and find the numbers using matrix method.
question_text: The sum of three numbers is 6. If we multiply third number by 3 and add second number to it, we get 11. By adding first and third numbers, we get double of the second number. Represent it algebraically and find the numbers using matrix method.
explanation: Let first ,second and third number be denoted by x,y and z, respectively.Then, according to given conditions,we have, x + y + z = 6 y + 3z = 11 x + z = 2y or x-2y+z=0 This system can be written as AX = B whose {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 0&1&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right|=1(1+6)-(0-3)+(0-1) = 9 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}{A_{11}} = 7,{A_{12}} = 3,{A_{13}} = - 1{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - 3,{A_{22}} = 0,{A_{23}} = 3{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = 2,{A_{32}} = - 3,{A_{33}} = 1{/tex} {tex}adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}}=\frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adjA = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 42-33+0 \\ 18+0+0 \\ -6+33+0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l}=\frac19\begin{bmatrix}9\\18\\27\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}1\\2\\3\end{bmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} Hence, x=1, y=2, z=3
solution: Let first ,second and third number be denoted by x,y and z, respectively.Then, according to given conditions,we have, x + y + z = 6 y + 3z = 11 x + z = 2y or x-2y+z=0 This system can be written as AX = B whose {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 0&1&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right|=1(1+6)-(0-3)+(0-1) = 9 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}{A_{11}} = 7,{A_{12}} = 3,{A_{13}} = - 1{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - 3,{A_{22}} = 0,{A_{23}} = 3{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = 2,{A_{32}} = - 3,{A_{33}} = 1{/tex} {tex}adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}}=\frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adjA = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 42-33+0 \\ 18+0+0 \\ -6+33+0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} = {tex}\begin{array}{l}=\frac19\begin{bmatrix}9\\18\\27\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}1\\2\\3\end{bmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} Hence, x=1, y=2, z=3
solution_html:
Let first ,second and third number be denoted by x,y and z, respectively.Then, according to given conditions,we have,
x + y + z = 6
y + 3z = 11
x + z = 2y or x-2y+z=0
This system can be written as AX = B whose

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 0&1&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left| A \right|=1(1+6)-(0-3)+(0-1) = 9 \ne 0{/tex}
{tex}{A_{11}} = 7,{A_{12}} = 3,{A_{13}} = - 1{/tex}

{tex}{A_{21}} = - 3,{A_{22}} = 0,{A_{23}} = 3{/tex}

{tex}{A_{31}} = 2,{A_{32}} = - 3,{A_{33}} = 1{/tex}

{tex}adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{A^{ - 1}}=\frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adjA = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&{ - 3}&2 \\ 3&0&{ - 3} \\ { - 1}&3&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ {11} \\ 0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{9}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 42-33+0 \\ 18+0+0 \\ -6+33+0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}={tex}\begin{array}{l}=\frac19\begin{bmatrix}9\\18\\27\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}1\\2\\3\end{bmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex}

Hence, x=1, y=2, z=3

question_html: The cost of 4kg onion, 3kg wheat and 2kg rice is Rs. 60. The cost of 2kg onion, 4kg wheat and 6kg rice is Rs. 90. The cost of 6kg onion 2kg wheat and 3kg rice is Rs. 70. Find the cost of each item per kg by matrix method.
question_text: The cost of 4kg onion, 3kg wheat and 2kg rice is Rs. 60. The cost of 2kg onion, 4kg wheat and 6kg rice is Rs. 90. The cost of 6kg onion 2kg wheat and 3kg rice is Rs. 70. Find the cost of each item per kg by matrix method.
explanation: Let cost of 1kg onion = x cost of 1kg wheat = y cost of 1kg rise = z By the question ,we have, 4x + 3y + 2z = 60 2x + 4y + 6z = 90 6x + 2y + 3z = 70 {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {60} \\ {90} \\ {70} \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right| = \left|{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right|= 50 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}Now,A_{11}=0,A_{12}=30,A_{13}=-20{/tex} {tex}A_{21}=-5,A_{22}=0,A_{23}=10{/tex} {tex}A_{31}=10,A_{32}=-20,A_{33}=10{/tex} {tex}\therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{50}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 5, y = 8, z = 8
solution: Let cost of 1kg onion = x cost of 1kg wheat = y cost of 1kg rise = z By the question ,we have, 4x + 3y + 2z = 60 2x + 4y + 6z = 90 6x + 2y + 3z = 70 {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {60} \\ {90} \\ {70} \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right| = \left|{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right|= 50 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}Now,A_{11}=0,A_{12}=30,A_{13}=-20{/tex} {tex}A_{21}=-5,A_{22}=0,A_{23}=10{/tex} {tex}A_{31}=10,A_{32}=-20,A_{33}=10{/tex} {tex}\therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{50}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 5, y = 8, z = 8
solution_html:
Let  cost of 1kg onion = x

cost of 1kg wheat = y

cost of 1kg rise = z

By the question ,we have,

4x + 3y + 2z = 60

2x + 4y + 6z = 90

6x + 2y + 3z = 70

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {60} \\ {90} \\ {70} \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left| A \right| = \left|{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right|= 50 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}Now,A_{11}=0,A_{12}=30,A_{13}=-20{/tex}

{tex}A_{21}=-5,A_{22}=0,A_{23}=10{/tex}

{tex}A_{31}=10,A_{32}=-20,A_{33}=10{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{50}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

x = 5, y = 8, z = 8

question_html: A total amount of Rs 7000 is deposited in three different savings bank accounts with annual interest rates of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} ,respectively. The total annual 2% interest from these three accounts is Rs 550. Equal amounts have been deposited in the 5% and 8% savings accounts. Find the amount deposited in each of the three accounts, with the help of matrices.
question_text: A total amount of Rs 7000 is deposited in three different savings bank accounts with annual interest rates of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} ,respectively. The total annual 2% interest from these three accounts is Rs 550. Equal amounts have been deposited in the 5% and 8% savings accounts. Find the amount deposited in each of the three accounts, with the help of matrices.
explanation: Let Rs x, Rs y and Rs z be invested in saving accounts at the rate of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} , respectively. Then, according to given condition ,we have the following system of equations x + y + z = 7000, ...(i) and {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 100 } + \frac { 8 y } { 100 } + \frac { 17 z } { 200 } = 550{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x + 16x + 17z = 110000 ...(ii) Also, x - y = 0 ...(iii) This system of equations can be written in matrix from as AX = B where, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] , X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] \text { and } B = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Here, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}| A | = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) + 1 ( - 10 - 16 ){/tex} = 17 +17 - 26 = 8 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 So, A is non- singular matrix and its inverse exists. Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are, {tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) = 17{/tex} {tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) = 17{/tex} {tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 16 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 10 - 16 ) = - 26{/tex} {tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 0 + 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 - 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex} {tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 16 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 17 - 16 ) = 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 17 - 10 ) = - 7{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 16 - 10 ) = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and the solution of given system is given by X = A -1 B. {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750. Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.

1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 10 - 16 ) = - 26{/tex} {tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 0 + 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 - 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex} {tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 16 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 17 - 16 ) = 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 17 - 10 ) = - 7{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 16 - 10 ) = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and the solution of given system is given by X = A -1 B. {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750. Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.
solution_html:
Let Rs x, Rs y and Rs z be invested in saving accounts at the rate of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex}, respectively.
Then, according to given condition ,we have the following system of equations
x + y + z = 7000, ...(i)
and {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 100 } + \frac { 8 y } { 100 } + \frac { 17 z } { 200 } = 550{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x + 16x + 17z = 110000 ...(ii)
Also, x - y = 0 ...(iii)
This system of equations can be written in matrix from as AX = B
where, A ={tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] , X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] \text { and } B = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Here, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}| A | = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) + 1 ( - 10 - 16 ){/tex}
= 17 +17 - 26 = 8 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
So, A is non- singular matrix and its inverse exists.
Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are,
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) = 17{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) = 17{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 16 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 10 - 16 ) = - 26{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 0 + 1 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 - 1 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 16 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 17 - 16 ) = 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 17 - 10 ) = - 7{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 16 - 10 ) = 6{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, A-1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and the solution of given system is given by
X = A-1 B.
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750.
Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 0 & -3 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations :
{tex}x+2 y-3 z=1{/tex}
{tex}2 x-3 z=2{/tex}
{tex}x+2 y=3{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 0 & -3 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations : {tex}x+2 y-3 z=1{/tex} {tex}2 x-3 z=2{/tex} {tex}x+2 y=3{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist {tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist {tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex}The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

solution_html:
Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right| = 15 - 14 = 1 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Matrix {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right| = 54 - 56 = - 2 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}adj.B = \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 8} \\ { - 7}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18 + 49}&{24 + 63} \\ {12 + 35}&{16 + 45} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {67}&{87} \\ {47}&{61} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| {AB} \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {67}&{87} \\ {47}&{61} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 67(61) - 87(47) = 4087 - 4089 {tex}= - 2 \ne 0{/tex}

Now L.H.S. = {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| {AB} \right|}}adj.\left( {AB} \right) {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {61}&{ - 87} \\ { - 47}&{67} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}….(i)

R.H.S. = B-1A-1 {tex} = \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 8} \\ { - 7}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {45 + 16}&{ - 63 - 24} \\ { - 35 - 12}&{49 + 18} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {61}&{ - 87} \\ { - 47}&{67} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}….(ii)

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i) and (ii), we get

L.H.S. = R.H.S.

{tex} \Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = {B^{ - 1}}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}
